text: When designing a REST API, clarity and consistency are key. Start by naming your endpoints with nouns, like `/users` or `/products`, instead of verbs. Use HTTP methods such as GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE to perform actions. Always return appropriate status codes: 200 for success, 201 for created resources, 400 for bad requests, and 404 for not found. Good documentation is essential—it helps developers understand how to use your API quickly. Finally, plan for changes by adding versioning from the start, for example, `/v1/users`. This allows you to update your API without breaking existing users' applications.
